docs.text: SudoBot # The ultimate solution for discord server moderation. Invite SudoBot # Read the terms here and if you agree with those then you can submit an invite request here . Features # Strong automoderation tools like Anti-raid systems and spam filters Various message filters A lot of manual moderation tools Secure
docs.title: Home

docs.text: The ultimate solution for discord server moderation.
docs.title: SudoBot

docs.text: Read the terms here and if you agree with those then you can submit an invite request here .
docs.title: Invite SudoBot

docs.text: Strong automoderation tools like Anti-raid systems and spam filters Various message filters A lot of manual moderation tools Secure
docs.title: Features

docs.text: Moderation # SudoBot has these manual moderation tools: Banning/Kicking members Softbanning/tempbanning/massbanning members Muting and hardmuting members Clearing messages in bulk Warning users Sending DMs to users Locking/unlocking channels Taking notes about users
docs.title: Moderation

docs.text: SudoBot has these manual moderation tools: Banning/Kicking members Softbanning/tempbanning/massbanning members Muting and hardmuting members Clearing messages in bulk Warning users Sending DMs to users Locking/unlocking channels Taking notes about users
docs.title: Moderation

docs.text: Banning Users # Normal Ban # Use the -ban command to ban a user, regardless if they are in the server or not. Legacy Command # -ban <UserID|UserMention|UserTag> [-d=DAYS] [Reason] Options : -d : Specify the how many days old messages to delete. Must be in range of 0-7. Default is - "Don't delete any". Slash Command # /ban <User> [days] [reason] Examples # -ban @Someone -ban @Someone Spamming a lot -ban @Someone -d 5 Spamming a lot -ban 45643846466843 -ban 45643846466843 Spamming a lot -ban 45643846466843 -d 7 Spamming a lot Mass Ban # Use the -massban command to massban users. Legacy Command # -massban <...UserIDs|UserMentions> [Reason] Slash Command # /massban <...UserIDs> [days] [reason] Note While using slash command, the IDs must be separated with spaces. Examples # -massban 23626825621964 217216291276407 -massban 23626825621964 217216291276407 63876523582826865 Raiding the Server -massban 23626825621964 217216291276407 @Someone 36362742746674467 Unbanning # Use the -unban command to unban users. Legacy Command # -unban <UserID> Slash Command # /unban <UserID> Examples # -unban 7485692162169
docs.title: Banning Users

docs.text: Use the -ban command to ban a user, regardless if they are in the server or not.
docs.title: Normal Ban

docs.text: -ban <UserID|UserMention|UserTag> [-d=DAYS] [Reason] Options : -d : Specify the how many days old messages to delete. Must be in range of 0-7. Default is - "Don't delete any".
docs.title: Legacy Command

docs.text: -ban @Someone -ban @Someone Spamming a lot -ban @Someone -d 5 Spamming a lot -ban 45643846466843 -ban 45643846466843 Spamming a lot -ban 45643846466843 -d 7 Spamming a lot
docs.title: Examples

docs.text: Use the -massban command to massban users.
docs.title: Mass Ban

docs.text: /massban <...UserIDs> [days] [reason] Note While using slash command, the IDs must be separated with spaces.
docs.title: Slash Command

docs.text: Use the -unban command to unban users.
docs.title: Unbanning

docs.text: Clearing messages in bulk # Sudobot has -clear command which can delete messages in bulk. Warning Messages deleted using this command won't be logged. Legacy Command # -clear <UserID|UserMention> Slash Command # /clear [User] [MessageCount] [Channel] Note While using slash command, you must specify either the message count or the user. Info This operation might take some time depending on the amount of messages. Examples # -clear @Someone -clear 347382275362482
docs.title: Clearing messages in bulk

docs.text: Sudobot has -clear command which can delete messages in bulk. Warning Messages deleted using this command won't be logged.
docs.title: Clearing messages in bulk

docs.text: /clear [User] [MessageCount] [Channel] Note While using slash command, you must specify either the message count or the user. Info This operation might take some time depending on the amount of messages.
docs.title: Slash Command

docs.text: Locking Channels # You can lock channels using -lock command. This sets a permission override to the target channel that denies SEND_MESSAGES permission for @everyone role. Warning If other roles have SEND_MESSAGES permission allowed then members having them could bypass the channel lock. You should take care about this. Legacy Command # -lock [ChannelID|ChannelMention] Slash Command # /lock [Channel] Note If the channel is not specified then the system will lock the current channel. Examples # -lock -lock 347382275362482 -lock #general Unlocking Channels # You can unlock channels back using -unlock command. This sets a permission override to the target channel that reverts the SEND_MESSAGES permission for @everyone role as it was before. Legacy Command # -unlock [ChannelID|ChannelMention] Slash Command # /unlock [Channel] Note If the channel is not specified then the system will unlock the current channel. Examples # -unlock -unlock 347382275362482 -unlock #general
docs.title: Locking Channels

docs.text: You can lock channels using -lock command. This sets a permission override to the target channel that denies SEND_MESSAGES permission for @everyone role. Warning If other roles have SEND_MESSAGES permission allowed then members having them could bypass the channel lock. You should take care about this.
docs.title: Locking Channels

docs.text: /lock [Channel] Note If the channel is not specified then the system will lock the current channel.
docs.title: Slash Command

docs.text: You can unlock channels back using -unlock command. This sets a permission override to the target channel that reverts the SEND_MESSAGES permission for @everyone role as it was before.
docs.title: Unlocking Channels

docs.text: /unlock [Channel] Note If the channel is not specified then the system will unlock the current channel.
docs.title: Slash Command

docs.text: Muting members # Use the -mute command to mute members. This will assign a muted role to the member and each channel is expected to have a permission override for this role which denies the SEND_MESSAGES permission. Legacy Command # -mute <UserID|UserMention|UserTag> [-t=TIME] [Reason] Slash Command # /mute <Member> [Time] [Reason] [Hardmute] Info Hardmuting means not only muting but also taking out all other roles except the muted role. They'll be given back when the unmute command is run or the mute duration expires. Examples # -mute @Someone -mute 347382275362482 -mute @Someone Spamming -mute @Someone -t 50m Spamming -mute 347382275362482 -t 12h Spamming Unmuting # Use the -unmute command to unmute members. Legacy Command # -unmute <UserID|UserMention|UserTag> Slash Command # /unmute <Member> Examples # -unmute @Someone -unmute 5256258251685246
docs.title: Muting members

docs.text: Use the -mute command to mute members. This will assign a muted role to the member and each channel is expected to have a permission override for this role which denies the SEND_MESSAGES permission.
docs.title: Muting members

docs.text: /mute <Member> [Time] [Reason] [Hardmute] Info Hardmuting means not only muting but also taking out all other roles except the muted role. They'll be given back when the unmute command is run or the mute duration expires.
docs.title: Slash Command

docs.text: Use the -unmute command to unmute members.
docs.title: Unmuting

docs.text: Other moderation commands # Kicking members # Legacy Command # -kick <UserID|UserMention|UserTag> [Reason] Slash Command # /kick <Member> [Reason] Examples # -kick @Someone -kick 34738227536248 -kick @Someone Spamming -kick 34738227536248 Spamming Beaning members # Beaning members actually do nothing except sending a message to the user that says You've been beaned . This can be used for fun purposes, or a moderation reminder. Legacy Command # -bean <UserID|UserMention|UserTag> [Reason] Slash Command # /bean <Member> [Reason] Examples # -bean @Someone -bean 34738227536248 -bean @Someone Spamming -bean 34738227536248 Spamming
docs.title: Other moderation commands

docs.text: Beaning members actually do nothing except sending a message to the user that says You've been beaned . This can be used for fun purposes, or a moderation reminder.
docs.title: Beaning members

docs.text: Taking notes for a user # Legacy Command # -note <UserID|UserMention|UserTag> <...NoteContent> Slash Command # /note <User> <Content> Examples # -note @Someone Something to note... -note 347382275362482 Something to note... Viewing notes # Legacy Command # -noteget <NoteID> Slash Command # /noteget <NoteID> Info Note IDs are shown when you create them. Also, running -notes command will list all notes for a user with their IDs. Examples # -noteget 3 Viewing all notes for a user # Legacy Command # -notes <UserID|UserMention|UserTag> Slash Command # /notes <User> Examples # -notes @Someone -notes 72926658752652945 Deleting notes # Legacy Command # -notedel <NoteID> Slash Command # /notedel <NoteID> Examples # -notedel 3
docs.title: Taking notes for a user

docs.text: -note @Someone Something to note... -note 347382275362482 Something to note...
docs.title: Examples

docs.text: /noteget <NoteID> Info Note IDs are shown when you create them. Also, running -notes command will list all notes for a user with their IDs.
docs.title: Slash Command
Viewing all notes for a user

docs.text: Warning members # Legacy Command # -warn <UserID|UserMention|UserTag> [Reason] Slash Command # /note <User> [Reason] Examples # -warn @Someone Do not spam -warn 347382275362482 Do not spam Deleting warnings # Legacy Command # -warning remove <ID> Slash Command # /warning remove <ID> Examples # -warning remove 3 Deleting all warnings for a user # Legacy Command # -warning clear <UserID|UserTag|UserMention> Slash Command # /warning clear <User> Examples # -warning clear @Someone Viewing warnings # Legacy Command # -warning view <ID> Slash Command # /warning view <ID> Examples # -warning view 3
docs.title: Warning members

docs.text: -warn @Someone Do not spam -warn 347382275362482 Do not spam
docs.title: Examples
Deleting all warnings for a user
